title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy suspended 6 games for slashing Sabres’ Zach Benson
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Charlie McAvoy suspended six games for slashing Zach Benson during Game 6 against the Buffalo Sabres.
Incident occurred during a 4-1 loss at TD Garden; McAvoy was penalized with a major for slashing and game misconduct.
This is McAvoy's third NHL suspension, following penalties in 2019 and 2023 for illegal head checks.

title: TD Bank doubles its space amid return-to-office push
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: TD Bank has signed a 10-year lease for nearly 39,000 square feet at 2 International Place in Boston.
The new office space is nearly double the size of its current location, accommodating up to 170 employees initially.
TD Bank's office expansion follows a 4-day in-office workweek policy, similar to recent moves by Fidelity and JP Morgan Chase.

title: What would AJ Brown bring to the Patriots if they land him? All-Pro DB Kevin Byard knows.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots are rumored to be interested in trading for Eagles receiver AJ Brown, former Titan.
Kevin Byard praised Brown's ability to attract defensive coverage, potentially benefiting other players.
Brown has surpassed 1,000 receiving yards in six of his first seven NFL seasons.

title: Celebrate America’s most painted building in Rockport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Motif No. 1 Day will be held on May 16 in Rockport, celebrating the iconic red fishing shack.
The festival features a 5K race, food, live music, plein air painters, and children's activities, expecting 2,000 attendees.
Motif No. 1 has appeared in movies, ads, and postage stamps, known for its picturesque New England setting.

title: Middleborough Dairy Queen sells for more than $4 million
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Middleborough Dairy Queen sold for $4.2 million to owner's nieces, Nicole Pennini Ferrara and Kara Pennini Kendrigan.
The location ranks second-highest in U.S. sales among 'Treat Only' Dairy Queens and is the most-visited in Massachusetts.
Originally opened in 1952, the store was owned by the Pennini family since 1966 and features a classic barn-like structure.

title: Kane’s Donuts debuts World Cup-themed doughnuts to crown fans’ favorite team
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Kane's Donuts introduces World Cup-themed doughnuts, starting with Haiti and Scotland designs ahead of their June matchup.
Doughnuts are decorated in each country's flag colors: Scotland's blue-and-white and Haiti's red-and-blue.
Kane's Donuts has locations in Saugus and Boston, offering new country-themed doughnuts weekly during the tournament.

title: Court docs: Suspected Cambridge gunman waved rifle at parole officer over FaceTime prior to shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Brown allegedly waved a rifle and made threats during a FaceTime call with his parole officer before the shooting.
Authorities were informed Brown relapsed and made suicidal threats before the Cambridge Memorial Drive shooting.
Brown, on probation for a 2020 shootout, allegedly shot two people and was injured by return fire from a civilian and trooper.

title: 2 N.H. residents were on cruise ship that quarantined over hantavirus, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two New Hampshire residents returned to the U.S. after being on the MV Hondius cruise ship during a hantavirus outbreak.
The outbreak resulted in three deaths, with one American testing positive but asymptomatic, according to the World Health Organization.
New Hampshire DHHS is coordinating with federal partners for residents' return and monitoring to ensure public health safety.

title: Red Sox might have to make a decision on Trevor Story amid veteran’s struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Trevor Story is struggling with a .200 average, 55 strikeouts, and five errors in the 2026 season.
The Red Sox may consider moving Story to second base and promoting Marcelo Mayer to shortstop.
Options for addressing Story's performance include reducing his at-bats, a salary dump trade, or a DFA move.

title: Lynn mother, educator avoids deportation for now
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mariola, a Lynn educator and mother from Guatemala, was granted a reprieve from deportation after meeting with ICE.
She has lived in the U.S. for 16 years, fleeing discrimination in Guatemala and overcoming significant challenges.
A 'Reasonable Fear Interview' will determine her eligibility for asylum; her next ICE check-in is in November.
